ACos.summary: Returns an angle corresponding to a particular cosine value

ASMSetAnimationRate.summary: change the delta animation rate for the entity using ASM.

ASin.summary: Returns an angle corresponding to a particular sin value

ATan.summary: Returns an angle corresponding to a particular tangential value

Abs.summary: Returns the absolute value of a float

AbsAngleClamp180.summary: Returns abs angle value clamped between [0,180].

AbsAngleClamp360.summary: Returns <angle> as a value in the range of 0 and 360 degrees.

ActionButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the 'action' button. Use this for when the player might be in a scripted sequence but might still press the jump button.

ActionSlotFourButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the action slot 4 button

ActionSlotOneButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the action slot 1 button

ActionSlotThreeButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the action slot 3 button

ActionSlotTwoButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the action slot 2 button

ActivateClientExploder.summary: Activates a client side exploderThis is sent as a reliable command, and will not work with killcam or demos.
ActivateClientExploder.mandatory1: <exploder id> id of exploder to activate

ActivateClientRadiantExploder.summary: Activates a client side radiant exploder
ActivateClientRadiantExploder.mandatory1: <exploder id> string id of exploder to activate

ActorIKEnabled.summary: Determines whether this actor can use IK functions. Returns true if IK is available, otherwise false.

AddBoltedFXExclusionVolume.summary: Create a bolted volume that will exclude FX that are flagged as "Respect Exclusion Volumes". Returns volume ID.
AddBoltedFXExclusionVolume.mandatory1: <localClientNum> The local client to create the volume on.
AddBoltedFXExclusionVolume.mandatory2: <entity> The entity to attach the volume to.
AddBoltedFXExclusionVolume.mandatory3: <tag name> The tag to use as the volume's origin.
AddBoltedFXExclusionVolume.mandatory4: <half extents> The half extents of the volume.
AddBoltedFXExclusionVolume.optional1: <offset> An offset from the tag name for the volume's origin; default is 0 0 0.

AddBonusCardStat.summary: Sets the indicated stat for that bonus card
AddBonusCardStat.mandatory2: <statname> The name of the stat you wish to set
AddBonusCardStat.mandatory3: <statincrease> The amount you would like to increase the stat
AddBonusCardStat.mandatory4: <currentclassnum> The loadout the player is using

AddDemoBookmark.summary: adds the information to the demo bookmarking system
AddDemoBookmark.example: AddDemoBookmark( bookmark_string, gettime(), self GetEntityNumber(), 255, 0, inflictorEntNum, inflictorEntType, inflictorBirthTime, false, self.grenade_multiattack_ent GetEntityNumber() )
AddDemoBookmark.mandatory2: <time> the time of recording the bookmark
AddDemoBookmark.mandatory3: <client1> the client related to the bookmark
AddDemoBookmark.mandatory4: <client2> other client which is associated with the bookmark

AddDuplicateRenderOption.summary: Add a duplicate render option for the entity
AddDuplicateRenderOption.example: myEnt AddDuplicateRenderOption( DR_TYPE_FRAMEBUFFER, DR_METHOD_THERMAL_MATERIAL, DR_CULL_ALWAYS )myEnt AddDuplicateRenderOption( DR_TYPE_OFFSCREEN, DR_METHOD_CUSTOM_MATERIAL, DR_CULL_NEVER, mapped_material_id(player,"gfx_my_material_2") )
AddDuplicateRenderOption.mandatory1: <dupeType> this is one of [DR_TYPE_FRAMEBUFFER|DR_TYPE_FRAMEBUFFER_DUPLICATE|DR_TYPE_OFFSCREEN], as defined in _duplicaterender.csc. Sets the duplicate type of this call
AddDuplicateRenderOption.mandatory2: <dupeMethod> this is one of [DR_CULL_ALWAYS|DR_CULL_NEVER], as defined in _duplicaterender.csc. Sets the render method of this call
AddDuplicateRenderOption.optional1: <mappedMaterialId> This is the mapped material id
AddDuplicateRenderOption.optional2: <dupeCull> this is one of [DR_METHOD_OFF|DR_METHOD_DEFAULT_MATERIAL|DR_METHOD_THERMAL_MATERIAL|DR_METHOD_CUSTOM_MATERIAL|DR_METHOD_FORCE_CUSTOM_MATERIAL], as defined in _duplicaterender.csc. Sets the render method of this call

AddEntityInfluencer.summary: Adds a new influencer of <name> attached to <entity&gt
AddEntityInfluencer.mandatory2: <entity> Attached to entity.  Goes away when entity is deleted. Unless a time is set.
AddEntityInfluencer.mandatory3: <team mask> teams that this influencer will affect

AddFXExclusionVolume.summary: Create a volume that will exclude FX that are flagged as "Respect Exclusion Volumes". Returns volume ID.
AddFXExclusionVolume.mandatory1: <localClientNum> The local client to create the volume on.
AddFXExclusionVolume.mandatory3: <angles> The rotation angles of the volume.
AddFXExclusionVolume.mandatory4: <half extents> The half extents of the volume.

AddFriendlyScrambler.summary: Adds new friendly scrambler to the compass
AddFriendlyScrambler.mandatory3: <handle> will be used to remove the scrambler (integer)

AddInfluencer.summary: Adds a new influencer of <name> at <origin&gt
AddInfluencer.mandatory3: <team mask> teams that this influencer will affect

AddOrientedInfluencer.summary: Adds a new influencer of <name> at <origin> and <angles&gt
AddOrientedInfluencer.example: influencer_id = AddOrientedInfluencer( name, origin, angles, allies_mask )
AddOrientedInfluencer.mandatory4: <team mask> teams that this influencer will affect

AddSensorGrenadeArea.summary: Adds new sensor grenade radius indicator to the compass
AddSensorGrenadeArea.mandatory2: <handle> will be used to remove the sensor

AddSentientEventListener.summary: Registers entity to listen to a specific event. Call waittill(<event>) to get the notification in script.

AddSpawnPoints.summary: Adds spawnpoints to the spawn system

AddTestClient.summary: Adds a test client to the map and returns a reference to that client.

AddToInterestPool.summary: Add a script event to the sentient's interest pool.
AddToInterestPool.mandatory1: <value> Amount the event adds to the pool. (integer)
AddToInterestPool.mandatory3: <lifespan> Time the event stays in the pool. (float)
AddToInterestPool.mandatory4: <point of interest> Position the AI will investigate. (vector3)
AddToInterestPool.optional1: [unique] Only one of this type can exist in the pool. (integer)

AddZombieBoxWeapon.mandatory2: <weaponModelName> The model name of the weapon
AddZombieBoxWeapon.mandatory3: <isDualWield> True if the weapon is dual wield, false otherwise

AdsButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the 'ads' button.

AimAtEntityIK.summary: Set this actor to aim at the specified entity. Call this function without any entity specified to turn it off.
AimAtEntityIK.optional1: [otherguy] the other guy to aim at

AimAtPosIK.summary: Set this actor to aim at the specified position. Call this function without any position specified to turn it off.

AimingAtFriendly.summary: Returns true if localclient 0 has crosshairs on friendly

AllClientsPrint.summary: Cause all clients to print the localized version of this string.

AllWeaponAttachmentsUnlocked.summary: Are all the weapon attachments unlocked for this weapon?

AllocateSoundRandoms.summary: Allocate a pool of random sounds

AllowActionSlotInput.mandatory1: <localClientNum> Client number of the controller you to enable the action slots

AllowAds.summary: Sets whether the player can switch to ADS
AllowAds.mandatory1: <player ads>: A boolean. true if the player can switch to ADS, and false otherwise.

AllowCrouch.summary: Sets whether the player can crouch
AllowCrouch.mandatory1: <player crouch>: A boolean. true if the player can crouch, and false otherwise

AllowDoubleJump.summary: Sets whether the player can double jump
AllowDoubleJump.mandatory1: <on off>: A boolean. true if the player can double jump, and false otherwise

AllowJump.summary: Sets whether the player can jump
AllowJump.mandatory1: <player jump>: A boolean. True if the player can jump, and false otherwise

AllowLean.summary: Sets whether the player can lean
AllowLean.mandatory1: <player lean>: A boolean. true if the player can lean, and false otherwise

AllowMelee.summary: Sets whether the player can melee
AllowMelee.mandatory1: <melee>: A boolean. True if the player can melee, and false otherwise

AllowMeleePowerLeft.summary: Sets whether the player can melee power left
AllowMeleePowerLeft.mandatory1: <melee>: A boolean. True if the player can melee power left, and false otherwise

AllowPitchAngle.summary: Allows AI to pitch on stairs

AllowProne.summary: Sets whether the player can go prone
AllowProne.mandatory1: <player prone>: A boolean. true if the player can go prone, and false otherwise

AllowRoundAnimation.summary: sets the allow round animation flag to enabled or disabled

AllowScoreboard.mandatory1: <localClientNum> Client number of the controller you to enable the action slots

AllowSlide.summary: Sets whether the player can slide
AllowSlide.mandatory1: <on off>: A boolean. true if the player can slide, and false otherwise

AllowSpectateTeam.summary: Set whether the player can spectate the given team.
AllowSpectateTeam.mandatory1: <team> A string description of the team. Valid teams are 'axis', 'allies', 'none' or 'freelook'
AllowSpectateTeam.mandatory2: <spectate> A boolean value describing whether this player can spectate on this team.

AllowSprint.summary: Sets whether the player can sprint
AllowSprint.mandatory1: <player sprint>: A boolean. True if the player can sprint, and false otherwise

AllowStand.summary: Sets whether the player can stand up
AllowStand.mandatory1: <player stand>: A boolean. true if the player can stand, and false otherwise

AllowTacticalInsertion.summary: If false, disallows tactical insertions in the bounding area of this entity. Allows tactical insertions otherwise

AllowWallRun.summary: Sets whether the player can wall run
AllowWallRun.mandatory1: <on off>: A boolean. true if the player can wallrun, and false otherwise

AllowedStances.summary: Sets the list of the actor's allowed stances.
AllowedStances.mandatory1: <stance> (string) A stance, can be 'prone', 'crouch', 'stand'. Any number of stances may be added.

AnglesToForward.summary: Returns the forward vector corresponding to a set of angles.

AnglesToRight.summary: Returns the right vector corresponding to a set of angles.

AnglesToUp.summary: Returns the up vector corresponding to a set of angles.

AnimHasNotetrack.summary: queries the given animation for a note track
AnimHasNotetrack.mandatory2: <note track> a constant string with the name of the note track

AnimMappingSearch.summary: Searches animation mapping table to return a animation name when given a animation mapping alias name.

AnimMode.summary: Set the way that animation deltas are interpreted by the game engine
AnimMode.mandatory1: <mode> which animmode to use. Must be 'gravity', 'nogravity', 'angle deltas', 'pos deltas', 'normal', 'normal_nogravity', 'zonly_physics', 'nophysics', 'none'

AnimRelative.summary: Runs an animscript on an entity.
AnimRelative.mandatory2: <origin> The starting position of the anim script
AnimRelative.mandatory3: <angles> The starting angle of the anim script

AnimScripted.summary: Runs an animscript on an entity.
AnimScripted.mandatory2: <origin> The starting position of the anim script
AnimScripted.mandatory3: <angles> The starting angle of the anim script
AnimScripted.optional4: [blend] The blend time for the animation
AnimScripted.optional5: [lerp] The lerp time for an aligned animation
AnimScripted.optional6: [animation time] The starting animation time in 0-1 range
AnimScripted.optional7: [is_scene_animation] Should be true for player scene animations or scripted animations where the player has no control, can be ignored for non-players
AnimScripted.optional8: [showPlayerWeaponInFirstPerson] determines if the first person player should have his weapon during cinematics

Announcement.summary: Sends an announcement to all clients.
Announcement.mandatory2: <duration> How long, in seconds, to display the announcement.

AnyAmmoForWeaponModes.summary: Returns true if the weapon has any ammo left

ApplyBallisticTarget.summary: Launch a vehicle with just enough force at a target.
ApplyBallisticTarget.mandatory1: <targetPos> The position to launch the vehicle towards

ApplyKnockBack.summary: Applies a knockback on a player.
ApplyKnockBack.mandatory1: <amount> the amount of knockback to apply
ApplyKnockBack.optional1: [dir] the direction of knockback to apply

AreAllMissionsAtScore.summary: Returns true if all missions are at the score specified
AreAllMissionsAtScore.mandatory1: <score> A score that all missions must be at to return true (integer)

AreControlsFrozen.summary: Returns true if the controls are frozen

AreMeshesStreamed.summary: Returns true if this entity's meshes are streamed in

AreTexturesLoaded.summary: Check to see if start-of-level textures are loaded - if they're not, the streamer forces a black screen.

AreTexturesStreamed.summary: Returns true if this entity's textures are streamed in

ArrayCombine.summary: combine two arrays into and returns one new array
ArrayCombine.mandatory3: <allowdupes> if true then allow duplicate entries, otherwise prune them out when combining
ArrayCombine.mandatory4: <preserve_keys> if true then preserve the keys (only for string keys) otherwise renumber when combining

ArrayCopy.summary: returns a copy of an array

ArrayGetClosest.summary: Returns the entity/vector that is closest to the <origin> from the <array>.
ArrayGetClosest.mandatory1: <origin> The origin to get closest from
ArrayGetClosest.optional1: [dist] The minimum distance to check when finding closest (has to be positive)

ArrayGetFarthest.summary: Returns the entity/vector that is farthest to the <origin> from the <array>.
ArrayGetFarthest.mandatory1: <origin> The origin to get farthest from
ArrayGetFarthest.optional1: [dist] The maximum distance when finding farthest (has to be positive)

ArrayInsert.summary: insert a value into an array at a given index
ArrayInsert.mandatory3: <index> index at which to insert the value (must be an integer) - moves existing entries as required

ArrayIntersect.summary: Returns an array of values that are present in both arrays.
ArrayIntersect.optional1: [matchKeys] Optionally returns an array of matching key/value pairs from the two input arrays (preserving the keys).

ArrayRemoveIndex.summary: Remove a value from an array at a given index
ArrayRemoveIndex.optional1: [preserve_keys] defaults to false, if true then it preserves the keys - otherwise creates integer keys

ArrayRemoveValue.summary: Remove a value from an array (may affect multiple indexes)
ArrayRemoveValue.optional1: [preserve_keys] defaults to false, if true then it preserves the keys - otherwise creates integer keys

ArraySort.summary: Returns a sorted array of entities by closest or farthest distance from <origin&gt
ArraySort.mandatory2: <origin> the origin from which to compare
ArraySort.optional1: [closest] if set to true the array will be sorted by distance closest to <origin>, if false by distance farthest from <origin>
ArraySort.optional2: [max] if set then only the first <max> count of entities will be returned
ArraySort.optional3: [range] if set then only the entities within <range> will be considered

ArraySortClosest.summary: Returns a sorted array of entities by closest from <origin&gt
ArraySortClosest.mandatory2: <origin> the origin from which to compare
ArraySortClosest.optional1: [<maxReturnCount] if set then only the first <max> count of entities will be returned
ArraySortClosest.optional2: [minDist] if set then only the entities further than <minDist> will be considered
ArraySortClosest.optional3: [maxDist] if set then only the entities closer then <maxDist will be considered

ArtilleryIconLocation.mandatory3: <isActive> Whether the icon is active or not
ArtilleryIconLocation.optional1: [isMortar] Whether the artillery is a mortar

Assert.summary: Assert that the given statement is correct. The function will throw a script error if this is false.
Assert.mandatory1: <value> statement that is asserted to be correct
Assert.optional1: [desc] string describing the nature of the assert

AssertMsg.summary: Throws a script error with the given message.

AtDangerousNode.summary: Determine whether this actor is near a dangerous node.

Attach.summary: Attach a model to an entity.
Attach.optional1: [tagname] The tag to attach the model to (string).
Attach.optional2: [ignoreCollision] flag to ignore collision. Defaults to false (integer).

AttachPath.summary: Attaches this vehicle to the given path.
AttachPath.mandatory1: <node> A node on the path to attach.

AttachShieldModel.summary: Attach a shield model to a player.
AttachShieldModel.optional1: [tagname] The tag to attach the model to (string).
AttachShieldModel.optional2: [ignoreCollision] flag to ignore collision. Defaults to false (integer).

AttachWeapon.summary: Attaches a weapon to the entity.
AttachWeapon.optional1: <renderoptions> The render options for the weapon

AttackButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the fire button

AttackedRecently.summary: Returns true if given sentient has attacked the entity within last "time" sec.

BadPlace_Box.summary: Creates a bad place box. AI will flee this position if they can, and will not go into it if they can avoid it.
BadPlace_Box.mandatory1: <name> The name of the bad place. If name is not "", the bad place can be moved or deleted by using the unique name.
BadPlace_Box.mandatory2: <duration> If duration > 0, the bad place will automatically delete itself after this time. If duration <= 0, the bad place must have a name and will last until manually deleted.
BadPlace_Box.mandatory5: <team> You must specify at least one team for which this place is bad, but can give several.  The allowed teams are 'axis', 'allies', and 'neutral'.

BadPlace_Cylinder.summary: Creates a bad place cylinder. AI will flee this position if they can, and will not go into it if they can avoid it.
BadPlace_Cylinder.mandatory1: <name> The name of the bad place. If name is not "", the bad place can be moved or deleted by using the unique name.
BadPlace_Cylinder.mandatory2: <duration> If duration > 0, the bad place will automatically delete itself after this time. If duration <= 0, the bad place must have a name and will last until manually deleted.
BadPlace_Cylinder.mandatory6: <team> You must specify at least one team for which this place is bad, but can give several.  The allowed teams are 'axis', 'allies', and 'neutral'.

BadPlace_Delete.summary: Deletes a bad place. It is okay to delete a bad place name that doesn't exist. It is not okay to delete the special name "".
BadPlace_Delete.mandatory1: <bad place identifier> The bad place to delete

BallSetTarget.summary: Sets the target of the ball to the given entity, and resets if no entity is specified.

Ban.mandatory1: <clientnum> The client number of the player to ban.

BeamKill.mandatory1: <localClientNum> Local client on which to play the beam

BeamLaunch.summary: Launch this beam and return the internal handle.
BeamLaunch.example: handle = BeamLaunch(0, launcher, launcherTag, target, targetTag, beam)
BeamLaunch.mandatory1: <localClientNum> Local client on which to play the beam
BeamLaunch.mandatory3: <tag from> The tag on the originating entity to start from

BeamTrace.summary: Performs a bullet trace between two points and returns an array of information about the trace result.
BeamTrace.mandatory1: <start_pos> (point) The start position for the trace.
BeamTrace.mandatory2: <end_pos> (point) The end position for the trace.
BeamTrace.mandatory3: <hit_characters> (bool) Tell the trace to hit characters or not.
BeamTrace.optional3: [ignore_ent2] (entity) Second entity to ingore for trace.

BeginLocationAirstrikeSelection.summary: Begin a location air strike selection

BeginLocationComlinkSelection.summary: Begin a location com link selection

BloodImpact.summary: Sets the blood impact type on the character
BloodImpact.mandatory1: <value> Has to be one of: "none", "hero" or "normal"

Bobbing.summary: Causes a script entity to bob around its origin, along a given vector dir
Bobbing.mandatory1: <direction vector> The direction of the bobbing
Bobbing.mandatory2: <amplitude> The amount of the bobbing in units
Bobbing.mandatory3: <period> The period of the bobbing in seconds
Bobbing.optional1: <phase> The phase offset of the bobbing

BonusCardActiveCount.summary: Returns how many bonus cards are active, cannot be called on a larry
BonusCardActiveCount.mandatory1: <classnum> The current class num of the player

BoostButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the change seat (Y) button

BotSetRandomCharacterCustomization.summary: Equips this class number with the default class

BoundsWouldTelefrag.summary: Returns true if the passed in bounding box would telefrag a player if another player was spawned there.
BoundsWouldTelefrag.example: if ( BoundsWouldTelefrag( vehicle GetAbsMins(), vehicle GetAbsmaxs() ) ) ...

BulletSpread.summary: uses spread to return a new end position

BulletTrace.summary: Performs a bullet trace between two points and returns an array of information about the trace result.
BulletTrace.mandatory1: <start_pos> (point) The start position for the trace.
BulletTrace.mandatory2: <end_pos> (point) The end position for the trace.
BulletTrace.mandatory3: <hit_characters> (bool) Tell the trace to hit characters or not.
BulletTrace.optional3: [ignore_ent2] (entity) Second entity to ingore for trace.

BulletTracePassed.fullAPI: BulletTracePassed(<start>,<end>,<hit characters>,<ignore entity>,[ignore entity 2],[fx vis],[ignore water])
BulletTracePassed.summary: Allows script to do a bullet point trace with ( MASK_SHOT & ~CONTENTS_SKY ) returns true if the trace does not hit anything
BulletTracePassed.example: passed = BulletTracePassed( grenade.origin, self.origin + (0,0,TROPHY_TRACE_Z), false, self )
BulletTracePassed.optional2: [fx vis] check against fx visibility also

BulletTracer.summary: creates a bullet tracer from the start to end point

ButtonBitState.summary: Return true if the button_bit(s) indicated are pressed (note:  there is no way to know exactly which buttons are pressed on the client, since everything is remapped into these bit values)
ButtonBitState.mandatory1: <button>: name of button to check "BUTTON_BIT_ATTACK" for example, optionally can include as many button names as desired.  Returns true if all buttons indicated are true.

ButtonPressed.summary: Check if the host is pressing the button/key, "BUTTON_A", "BUTTON_B", "K", etc...

CalcApproximatePathToPosition.summary: Returns an array of world positions representing the approximate path an actor will take.  Calculating an approximate path will cause the AI to clear their current path.
CalcApproximatePathToPosition.mandatory1: <position> The goal position for the path
CalcApproximatePathToPosition.optional1: [clear path] True to clear path, false otherwise. Defaults to True

CalcLookaheadPos.summary: Calculate a lookahead point based on a given starting location and number of iterations. Returns an array with these keys: "position", "node" and "next_node", which contain vectors for the actual lookahead position and the next two nodes on the path, respectively.
CalcLookaheadPos.mandatory1: <startPos> Starting position of the lookahead traces
CalcLookaheadPos.mandatory2: <numIter> Number of times to iterate (more gives better results, but is more expensive). Set to 0 to get current lookahead.

CalcPlayerOptions.mandatory1: <custom class slot> The custom class slot

CalcWeaponOptions.mandatory1: <custom class slot> The custom class slot
CalcWeaponOptions.mandatory2: <weapon slot> primary weapon=0 or secondary weapon=1 or knife weapon=2

CamAnimScripted.summary: Plays the camera script on a player.
CamAnimScripted.mandatory1: <player> The player to play animation on
CamAnimScripted.mandatory3: <start_time> The start time of the animation
CamAnimScripted.optional3: [ignoreProcessingInitialNoteTracks] Ignores processing the initial notetracks if the camera time starts earlier

CameraActivate.summary: Turns the script camera on or off.
CameraActivate.mandatory1: <active> True to activate the camera, false to turn it off and return to 1st person view

CameraForceDisableScriptCam.summary: Disable the script cam from client script.

CameraSetAngles.summary: Sets the focus point of the script camera.
CameraSetAngles.optional1: [position] Either an entity to focus the camera on or a vector defining the camera's focus point

CameraSetLookAt.summary: Sets the focus point of the script camera.
CameraSetLookAt.optional1: [position] Either an entity to focus the camera on or a vector defining the camera's focus point

CameraSetPosition.summary: Sets the position of the script camera.
CameraSetPosition.mandatory1: <position> Either an entity to link the camera to or a vector defining the world position
CameraSetPosition.optional1: [angles] The angles to set for the camera

CameraSetUpdateCallback.summary: Set the function that will get called during the camera update
CameraSetUpdateCallback.optional1: [function] a function pointer to a function that will get called during the camera update, the function should set the scr cam

CanAttackEnemyNode.summary: Checks if this actor can attack its enemies node.

CanClaimNode.summary: Returns true if the node is unclaimed, false otherwise.

CanPath.summary: Check if a path can be found between these positions.

CanPlaceRiotshield.summary: Try to place riotshield where the player is standing.  return an array containing placement "result", "origin", and "angles

CanPlayerPlaceTurret.summary: Try to place a Turret where the player is looking.  It'll return an array containing the placement "result", "origin", and "angles".

CanPlayerPlaceVehicle.summary: Try to place a vehicle in front of the player.  It'll return an array containing the placement "result", "origin", and "angles".
CanPlayerPlaceVehicle.mandatory3: <forward Distance> Distance away from player origin.
CanPlayerPlaceVehicle.mandatory4: <up Distance> Distance up from player origin.
CanPlayerPlaceVehicle.mandatory6: <min normal> Minimum hit normal needed to be pass.

CanSee.summary: Check to see if the AI can see the given entity.

CanShoot.summary: Check to see if the actor can shoot the given position.
CanShoot.mandatory1: <shoot at position> (vector) The position to shoot at.
CanShoot.optional1: [offset from gun] (vector) The offset from the gun muzzle from which to calculate collision.

CanShootEnemy.summary: Check to see if the actor can shoot his current enemy.
CanShootEnemy.optional1: [cache duration] (int) When set, uses a custom time value for the cache.

CanThrowGrenade.summary: Checks whether the actor can throw a grenade at a given position. If he can't then result will be undefined, otherwise the result is the resultant velocity vector
CanThrowGrenade.mandatory1: <hand offset> (vector) the estimated offset of the hand for the throw
CanThrowGrenade.mandatory2: <random range> (float) random range from target position to throw at

CanThrowGrenadePos.summary: Checks whether the actor can throw a grenade at a given position. If he can't then result will be undefined, otherwise the result is the resultant velocity vector
CanThrowGrenadePos.mandatory1: <hand offset> (vector) the estimated offset of the hand for the throw
CanThrowGrenadePos.mandatory2: <targetpos> (vector) The target position, should be at ground level of potential target

CancelAIMove.summary: Tell the vehicle to stop moving towards its current goal position.

CantSeeEntities.summary: Returns the set of entities that a player cannot see within a given angle, given a set of entities
CantSeeEntities.example: if( isplayer( self.enemy ) && self.enemy CantSeeEntities( entArray, 67 ) )
CantSeeEntities.mandatory2: <dotRange> The cosine of the angle representing your fov

CaptureFrame.summary: Captures the current frame into the given code image - he image had to be already created with CreateSceneCodeImage()

CarryTurret.summary: Links the auto turret to the player to carry around.
CarryTurret.mandatory2: <offset> the offset from the player to place the turret
CarryTurret.mandatory3: <angleOffset> the angle offset of the turret

Ceil.summary: Returns the ceiling of floating point value <a>. This effectively rounds up Ceil(2.7) == 3, Ceil(3.9) == 4.

ChangeFontScaleOverTime.summary: Set a hud element to transition in fontScale over time.  Normally setting the fontScale of an element causes an immediate visual change. However, if the fontScale gets set within <time> after calling ChangeFontScaleOverTime, then the hud element will transition to the new fontScale over the remaining <time>.
ChangeFontScaleOverTime.example: level.introstring1 ChangeFontScaleOverTime( 1.2 );  level.introstring1.fontScale = 0.3;  // This will transition the fontScale from whatever it was before to the new value of 0.3 over 1.2 seconds.
ChangeFontScaleOverTime.mandatory1: <time> The time to scale the element font in seconds

ChangeSeatButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the change seat (Y) button

CheckGrenadeThrow.summary: Checks whether the actor can throw a grenade at his target. If he can't then result will be undefined, otherwise the result is the resultant velocity vector
CheckGrenadeThrow.mandatory1: <hand offset> (vector) the estimated offset of the hand for the throw
CheckGrenadeThrow.mandatory2: <method> (string) The grenade toss method. Can be 'min energy', 'min time', and 'max tune'
CheckGrenadeThrow.mandatory3: <random range> (float) random range from target position to throw at

CheckGrenadeThrowPos.summary: Checks whether the actor can throw a grenade at a given position. If he can't then result will be undefined, otherwise the result is the resultant velocity vector

LinkToUpdateOffset.summary: Attaches one entity to another, gradually lerping entity to the parents orientation

LinkTraversal.summary: Creates a user edge connecting two path nodes

LoadSentientEventParameters.summary: Loads the global sentient event parameters for a given scriptbundle sentient events asset. (all AI will use these)
LoadSentientEventParameters.mandatory1: <scriptBundle> String name of the script bundle to load

LoadSiegeAnim.summary: Load a siege anim into memory
LoadSiegeAnim.mandatory1: <anim_name> Name of the siege anim to load

LocalClientActive.summary: Return whether local client is active
LocalClientActive.mandatory1: <client> the client to check if it is active

LocalToWorldCoords.summary: Transform the given local coordinate point to a world coordinate point
LocalToWorldCoords.mandatory1: <local coordinate> The point in local coordinates (vector3)

Log.summary: Returns the logarithm of a number to base 10

LookAtEntity.summary: Set this actor to look at the specified entity. Call this function without any entity specified to turn it off.
LookAtEntity.optional1: [otherguy] the other guy to look at

LookAtPos.summary: Set this actor to look at the specified position. Call this function without any position specified to turn it off.

LuiDisable.summary: user input is removed from lui system

LuiEnable.summary: user input is handed to lui system until returned
LuiEnable.mandatory2: <menuname> The name of the menu to enable

LuiLoad.summary: Loads a lui file, making the menus available for addition

MagicBullet.summary: Creates a magic bullet at the source
MagicBullet.example: MagicBullet( GetWeapon("sniper_hyperion"), level.sniper_loc.origin, target GetTagOrigin( "tag_eye" ), level.sniper_boss)
MagicBullet.mandatory2: <source> Where the bullet will be spawned
MagicBullet.mandatory3: <destination> Where the bullet will be fired at
MagicBullet.optional1: [attacker] Set the owner of the bullet to this
MagicBullet.optional3: [targetOffset] Offset the target by this vector

MagicGrenade.summary: Creates a "Magic" grenade from an actor.
MagicGrenade.mandatory1: <origin> The starting point of the grenade toss
MagicGrenade.mandatory2: <target position> The target point of the grenade toss
MagicGrenade.optional1: [time to blow] The grenade fuse time in seconds
MagicGrenade.optional2: [weapon] The grenade weapon to use (defaults to equipped grenade weapon)

MagicGrenadeManual.summary: creates a "Magic" grenade from an actor
MagicGrenadeManual.mandatory1: <origin> The starting point of the grenade toss
MagicGrenadeManual.mandatory2: <velocity> The initial velocity vector for the grenade movement
MagicGrenadeManual.optional1: [time to blow] The grenade fuse time in seconds

MagicGrenadeManualPlayer.summary: creates a "Magic" grenade from a player
MagicGrenadeManualPlayer.mandatory1: <origin> The starting point of the grenade toss
MagicGrenadeManualPlayer.mandatory2: <velocity> The initial velocity vector for the grenade movement
MagicGrenadeManualPlayer.mandatory3: <weapon> The grenade weapon to base on
MagicGrenadeManualPlayer.optional1: [time to blow] The grenade fuse time in seconds

MagicGrenadePlayer.summary: Creates a "Magic" grenade from a player.
MagicGrenadePlayer.mandatory2: <origin> The starting point of the grenade toss
MagicGrenadePlayer.mandatory3: <velocity> The initial velocity vector for the grenade movement

MagicGrenadeType.summary: Fire a 'magic grenade', from the source location towards the destination point.
MagicGrenadeType.optional1: [time to blow] The grenade fuse time in seconds

MagicMissile.summary: Launches a weapon from the given position with the given velocity.  Velocity determines direction.

MakeFakeAI.summary: Create a drone from script model, that can be moved around with simple commands

MakeGrenadeDud.summary: Turns a grenade into a dud

MakeLocalizedString.summary: Make a localized string of the given string

MakePathfinder.summary: Register a vehicle with Havok so it can do pathfinding and position query

MakeSentient.summary: Turn a vehicle or script mover into a sentient.

MakeTurretUnusable.summary: Sets a turret to be unable to be used

MakeTurretUsable.summary: Sets a turret able to be used

MakeUnusable.summary: Sets this entity to be not usable by the player

MakeUsable.summary: Sets this entity to be usable by the player
MakeUsable.optional1: [team] The team that is allowed to use the object. Only for script movers.

MakeVehicleUnusable.summary: Sets this vehicle to be not usable by the player

MakeVehicleUsable.summary: Sets this vehicle to be usable by the player

Map.optional1: [save persistent] if true then player info is retained

MapExists.summary: Returns true if the map with the given name exists on the server

MapFloat.summary: Returns a lerped value between <from> and <to> based on <time>. <time> is a value between <timeMin> and <timeMax>.
MapFloat.mandatory1: <timeMin> Floating point value, min time value
MapFloat.mandatory2: <timeMax> Floating point value, max time value
MapFloat.mandatory3: <from> Floating point value, start of the result range
MapFloat.mandatory4: <to> Floating point value, end of the result range
MapFloat.mandatory5: <time> Floating point value in the range from <timeMin> to <timeMax>

MapMaterialIndex.summary: Maps the material to an index
MapMaterialIndex.mandatory1: <localClientNum> Local client on which to map the material

MapShaderConstant.summary: Maps a shader constant to an index. Returns 1 if success, 0 on failure
MapShaderConstant.mandatory1: <localClientNum> Local client for which to map the shader constant
MapShaderConstant.mandatory2: <index> the index you want this shader constant to map to
MapShaderConstant.mandatory3: <constant name> a string that's the name of the shader constant

Map_Restart.optional1: [save persistent] if true then player info is retained

MarkNoVehicleNavMeshFaces.summary: Update the novehicle flag on navmesh faces. This should only be used in MP map for now.

Max.summary: Returns whichever of <a> or <b> is larger.

MayMoveFromPointToPoint.summary: Check whether the actor can move from a given point to a given point. Does not check friendly fire and in goal radius unlike maymovetopoint
MayMoveFromPointToPoint.optional1: [check drop] checks if the point would cause the AI to drop. Defaults to true.
MayMoveFromPointToPoint.optional2: [check direct] checks if there are no obstructions between actor origin and <Position>.

MayMoveToPoint.summary: Check whether the actor can move to a given point.
MayMoveToPoint.mandatory1: <position> (point) The position that the actor may be able to move to.
MayMoveToPoint.optional1: [check drop] checks if the point would cause the AI to drop. Defaults to true.
MayMoveToPoint.optional2: [check direct] checks if there are no obstructions between actor origin and <Position>.

Melee.summary: Makes this actor melee attack. Returns the entity hit, if any.
Melee.optional1: [direction] direction to attack, if not specified, uses direction to current enemy.

MeleeButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the melee button

MeleeWithOffset.summary: Makes this actor melee attack. Returns the entity hit, if any.
MeleeWithOffset.mandatory1: <offset> A vector offset added to the actor's eye position from which to start the melee trace.

Min.summary: Returns whichever of <a> or <b> is smallest.

Missile_CreateAttractorEnt.summary: Creates a missile attractor at the origin of an entity
Missile_CreateAttractorEnt.mandatory1: <entity> The entity that is to be an attractor
Missile_CreateAttractorEnt.mandatory2: <strength> The strength of the attractor (maximum acceleration it can apply to a missile)
Missile_CreateAttractorEnt.mandatory3: <affectdist> The effective distance at which the missile can be affected.  The effect of the attractor falls off from <strength> to zero from the center to this distance away from the center.
Missile_CreateAttractorEnt.optional1: <affectAllProjectiles> If set it will affect all projectiles inculding grenades and guided missiles.
Missile_CreateAttractorEnt.optional2: <alert range> If set it send notify when missile breaches range(but will not repulse).

Missile_CreateAttractorOrigin.summary: Creates a missile attractor at the given location
Missile_CreateAttractorOrigin.mandatory2: <strength> The strength of the attractor (maximum acceleration it can apply to a missile)
Missile_CreateAttractorOrigin.mandatory3: <affectDist> The effective distance at which the missile can be affected.  The effect of the attractor falls off from <strength> to zero from the center to this distance away from the center.
Missile_CreateAttractorOrigin.optional1: <affectAllProjectiles> If set it will affect all projectiles inculding grenades and guided missiles.
Missile_CreateAttractorOrigin.optional2: <alert range> If set it send notify when missile breaches range(but will not repulse).

Missile_CreateRepulsorEnt.summary: Creates a missile repulsor at the origin of an entity
Missile_CreateRepulsorEnt.mandatory1: <entity> The entity that is to be a repulsor
Missile_CreateRepulsorEnt.mandatory2: <strength> The strength of the repulsor (maximum acceleration it can apply to a missile)
Missile_CreateRepulsorEnt.mandatory3: <affectDist> The effective distance at which the missile can be affected.  The effect of the attractor falls off from <strength> to zero from the center to this distance away from the center.
Missile_CreateRepulsorEnt.optional1: [affectAllProjectiles] If set it will affect all projectiles inculding grenades and guided missiles.
Missile_CreateRepulsorEnt.optional2: [alert range] If set it send notify when missile breaches range(but will not repulse).

Missile_CreateRepulsorOrigin.summary: Creates a missile Repulsor at the given location
Missile_CreateRepulsorOrigin.mandatory2: <strength> The strength of the Repulsor (maximum acceleration it can apply to a missile)
Missile_CreateRepulsorOrigin.mandatory3: <affectDist> The effective distance at which the missile can be affected.  The effect of the attractor falls off from <strength> to zero from the center to this distance away from the center.
Missile_CreateRepulsorOrigin.optional1: [affectAllProjectiles] If set it will affect all projectiles inculding grenades and guided missiles.
Missile_CreateRepulsorOrigin.optional2: [alert range] If set it send notify when missile breaches range(but will not repulse).

Missile_DeleteAttractor.summary: Deletes a missile attractor or repulsor

Missile_DroneSetVisible.summary: Set the missile or drone to be visible
Missile_DroneSetVisible.mandatory1: <flag> True to set visible, false otherwise

Missile_GetTarget.summary: Sets the target of the missile to the given entity, and resets if no entity is specified.

MissionHasAccolades.summary: Returns whether or not this mission has any accolades associated with it
MissionHasAccolades.optional1: [currentmap] the name of the current map.  If ommitted, use Dvar sv_mapname

MissionHasCollectibles.summary: Returns whether or not this mission has any collectibles in it
MissionHasCollectibles.optional1: [currentmap] the name of the current map.  If ommitted, use Dvar sv_mapname

MissionRestart.summary: Restarts the mission, which might take you to a different BSP if this is a sublevel

MotionPulse_Enable.summary: Enable or disable the sitrep scan

MotionPulse_SetDesat.summary: Set the motion pulse desaturation level

MotionPulse_SetLineWidth.summary: Set the motion pulse line width

MotionPulse_SetOutline.summary: Set the motion pulse outline level

MotionPulse_SetSolid.summary: Set the motion pulse solid level

MoveGravity.mandatory1: <initial velocity> The initial velocity to fling this entity at
MoveGravity.mandatory2: <time> The time to move the entity in seconds

MoveOverTime.summary: Set a hud element to move over time.
MoveOverTime.mandatory1: <time> The time to move the element in seconds

MoveSlide.summary: Launches the entity in the given velocity. When on the ground it will slide smoothly. Call StopMoveSlide to make it stop moving.
MoveSlide.mandatory1: <center> The center of the entity's collision with the ground
MoveSlide.mandatory2: <radius> The radius of the entity's collision with the ground
MoveSlide.mandatory3: <initial velocity> The initial velocity to move the entity at

MoveTo.summary: Move this entity to the given point.
MoveTo.mandatory1: <point> The point to move the entity to
MoveTo.mandatory2: <time> The time to move the entity in seconds

MoveX.summary: Move this entity to the given world x value
MoveX.mandatory1: <point> The x value to move the entity to, as a floating point number
MoveX.mandatory2: <time> The time to move the entity in seconds
MoveX.optional1: [acceleration time] The time spent accelerating in seconds
MoveX.optional2: [deceleration time] The time spent decelerating in seconds

MoveY.summary: move this entity to the given world y value
MoveY.mandatory1: <point> The y value to move the entity to, as a floating point number
MoveY.mandatory2: <time> The time to move the entity in seconds
MoveY.optional1: [acceleration time] The time spent accelerating in seconds
MoveY.optional2: [deceleration time] The time spent decelerating in seconds

MoveZ.summary: Move this entity to the given world z value
MoveZ.mandatory1: <point> The z value to move the entity to, as a floating point number
MoveZ.mandatory2: <time> The time to move the entity in seconds
MoveZ.optional1: [acceleration time] The time spent accelerating in seconds
MoveZ.optional2: [deceleration time] The time spent decelerating in seconds

MurderLine.summary: Get all the enemy players on the line between given points and murder them to death

NavPointSightFilter.summary: Given an array of navmesh points, filters the array based on navmesh visibility to an origin.
NavPointSightFilter.optional1: [checksight] True to check sight, false otherwise

NearClaimNode.summary: Determine whether this actor is near its claim node.

NearClaimNodeAndAngle.summary: Determine whether this actor is near its claim node and nearly matches the angle.

NearNode.summary: Determine whether this actor is near to a given path node.

NeedsRevive.summary: Used for mini maps to display compass icon
NeedsRevive.mandatory1: <bool> does player need to be reived

NewClientHudElem.summary: Create a new hud element for a particular client
NewClientHudElem.mandatory1: <client> The client for whom the hud element is created.

NewDamageIndicatorHudElem.summary: Create a new hud element for a particular client
NewDamageIndicatorHudElem.mandatory1: <client> The client for whom the hud element is created.

NewScoreHudElem.summary: Create a new score hud element for a particular client
NewScoreHudElem.mandatory1: <client> The client for whom the hud element is created.

NewTeamHudElem.summary: Create a new hud element for a particular team
NewTeamHudElem.mandatory1: <team> The team for whom the hud element is created. Must be 'axis', 'allies' or 'spectator'

NextPlayerRenderOption.summary: Set the next player render option
NextPlayerRenderOption.mandatory1: <optionName> The tag of the next option

NodesAreLinked.summary: Returns true if node1 is connected to node2.

NodesVisible.summary: Returns true if these nodes are visible to one another, false otherwise.

NotSolid.summary: Unsets the solid flag, so that this object is no longer collidable.

NumRemoteClients.summary: Returns the number of remote clients connected to the game.  Very useful for scaling content for coop play, and network balancing.

OED_NightVision_SetTextureLevel.summary: Set the night vision texture level for the entity

OED_SitRepScan_Enable.summary: Enable or disable the sitrep scan

OED_SitRepScan_SetDesat.summary: Set the desaturation level for the sitrep scan

OED_SitRepScan_SetFalloff.summary: Set the falloff for the sitrep scan

OED_SitRepScan_SetLineWidth.summary: Set the line width for the sitrep scan

OED_SitRepScan_SetOutline.summary: Set the outline level for the sitrep scan

OED_SitRepScan_SetRadius.summary: Set the radius for the sitrep scan

OED_SitRepScan_SetSolid.summary: Set the solid level for the sitrep scan

Obituary.summary: Create an obituary for a character
Obituary.mandatory4: <weapon> The means of death as a string

Objective_Add.summary: Add a new objective, with ability to specify all the info for the objective
Objective_Add.mandatory1: <objective_number> The number of the objective to add
Objective_Add.mandatory2: <state> A string value representing the state of the objective. Valid states are "empty", "active", "invisible", "done", "current" and "failed"
Objective_Add.optional1: [position/onEntity] The position of the objective - or - <entity> entity to be tracked.
Objective_Add.optional2: [displayName] The text to use for the objective. This should be a valid localized text reference

Objective_ClearAllUsing.summary: Clears the objective of all players using status.
Objective_ClearAllUsing.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_ClearEntity.summary: Clears the entity previous set on the objective
Objective_ClearEntity.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_ClearPlayerUsing.summary: Sets the objective to be not being used by the specified player.
Objective_ClearPlayerUsing.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_ClearPlayerUsing.mandatory2: <player> The player who is no longer using the objective

Objective_Delete.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_FlipIcon.summary: Flips the icon of the objective
Objective_FlipIcon.optional1: [shouldFlipMaterial] 1 if the material should be flipped, 0 otherwise

Objective_GetGamemodeFlags.summary: Gets the gamemode flags for the objective.

Objective_Icon.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_OnEntity.summary: Sets the objective to get its position from an entity.
Objective_OnEntity.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_OnEntity.mandatory2: <entity> The entity to set the objective to

Objective_Position.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_Set3D.summary: Sets flag determining if 3D state for an objective is drawn.
Objective_Set3D.example: Objective_Set3D( myObjNum, true, undefined, undefined, undefined, (0,0,70) )
Objective_Set3D.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_Set3D.mandatory2: <use3D> true if should render in hud

Objective_SetColor.summary: Sets a color for the objective that the icon will then be drawn with.
Objective_SetColor.mandatory2: <r/colorName> Red float value for objective, or color name set from the UIEditor as an IString.
Objective_SetColor.optional3: [a] Alpha float value for objective. Default of 1.0 if not specified.

Objective_SetFlag.summary: Sets the gamemode flags for the objective.
Objective_SetFlag.mandatory1: <objective_index> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetFlag.mandatory2: <flag> - active, fadeoutonscreen, perk, drawdistance, drawname, in3d, clipToMap, primary
Objective_SetFlag.mandatory3: <true/false> turn the flag on or off

Objective_SetGamemodeFlags.summary: Sets the gamemode flags for the objective.  This function only works for modes not using 3d objectives (mp).

Objective_SetIcon.summary: Sets the icon for the objective
Objective_SetIcon.mandatory1: <localClientNum> The local client that is using the objective
Objective_SetIcon.mandatory2: <objectiveNumber> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_SetIconSize.summary: Sets the objective's scale for the icon.
Objective_SetIconSize.mandatory1: <localClientNum> The local client that is using the objective
Objective_SetIconSize.mandatory2: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetIconSize.mandatory3: <scale> The value to scale the objective icon

Objective_SetInvisibleToAll.summary: Sets the objective to be invisible to all players.
Objective_SetInvisibleToAll.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_SetInvisibleToPlayer.summary: Sets the objective to be invisible to the specified player.
Objective_SetInvisibleToPlayer.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetInvisibleToPlayer.mandatory2: <player> The player to whom the objective is now invisible

Objective_SetInvisibleToPlayerByIndex.summary: Sets the objective to be invisible to the specified player.
Objective_SetInvisibleToPlayerByIndex.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetInvisibleToPlayerByIndex.mandatory2: <entnum> The entity number of the player to whom the objective is now invisible

Objective_SetPlayerUsing.summary: Sets the objective to being used by the specified player.
Objective_SetPlayerUsing.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetPlayerUsing.mandatory2: <player> The player who is using the objective

Objective_SetProgress.summary: Sets the progress for the objective.

Objective_SetSize.summary: Sets a size for the objective that the icon will then be drawn at. Min is MIN_OBJECTIVE_ICON_SIZE = 5
Objective_SetSize.mandatory2: <entity/float> The entity to get the size from or a sizeX.
Objective_SetSize.optional1: [float] If sizeX was used in prvious arg, this is sizeY.

Objective_SetStencil.summary: Sets the objective's stencil for the icon.
Objective_SetStencil.mandatory1: <localClientNum> The local client that is using the objective
Objective_SetStencil.mandatory2: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetStencil.mandatory3: <stencil> Set to true if you want the objective to stencil

Objective_SetUIModelValue.fullAPI: Objective_SetUIModelValue(<objective_number>,<lui menu data name>,<lui menu data value>)
Objective_SetUIModelValue.summary: Set a UI Model Value for an objective
Objective_SetUIModelValue.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetUIModelValue.mandatory2: <lui menu data name> precached name string for the data which we're trying to set.
Objective_SetUIModelValue.mandatory3: <lui menu data value> value for the data which we're trying to set on the objective.

Objective_SetVisibleToAll.summary: Sets the objective to be visible to all players.
Objective_SetVisibleToAll.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_SetVisibleToPlayer.summary: Sets the objective to be visible to the specified player.
Objective_SetVisibleToPlayer.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetVisibleToPlayer.mandatory2: <player> The player to whom the objective is now visible

Objective_SetVisibleToPlayerByIndex.summary: Sets the objective to be visible to the specified player.
Objective_SetVisibleToPlayerByIndex.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_SetVisibleToPlayerByIndex.mandatory2: <entnum> The entity number of the player to whom the objective is now visible

Objective_State.summary: Set a state for the objective
Objective_State.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter

Objective_Team.summary: Sets the team that the objective is for. Allows having different objectives for each team
Objective_Team.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_Team.mandatory2: <team> The team that the objective is for. Valid entries are 'allies', 'axis', 'team3', or 'none'

Objective_VisibleTeams.summary: Sets the team that the objective is visible too for. Allows having different objectives for each team
Objective_VisibleTeams.mandatory1: <objective_number> The ID of the objective to alter
Objective_VisibleTeams.mandatory2: <team> The team that the objective is for. Valid entries are 'allies', 'axis', 'team3', or 'none'

OffhandSpecialButtonPressed.summary: Check if the player is pressing the gadget button

OffhandWeaponsEnabled.summary: Determine if offhand weapons are enabled for the player

OpenLUIMenu.summary: Opens a lui menu( has to be precahced ) and returns a handle to it.
OpenLUIMenu.mandatory1: <lui menu name> name of the menu.
OpenLUIMenu.optional1: [persistent] the menu will remain open even if the player dies.

OpenLUIPopup.mandatory1: <localClientNum> Number of the local client on the machine.
OpenLUIPopup.mandatory2: <name> Name of the popup menu to open.

OpenMenu.summary: Open the menu with the given menu index

OpenMenuNoMouse.summary: Open the menu with the given menu index

OrientMode.summary: Set the orient mode of this actor.
OrientMode.mandatory1: <orient mode> (const string) OrientMode must be 'face angle', 'face current', 'face direction', 'face enemy', 'face enemy or motion', 'face goal', 'face motion', 'face point', or 'face default'
OrientMode.optional1: [face angle] (float) The yaw angle to face if orient mode is 'face angle'
OrientMode.optional2: [direction vector] (vector) The direction vector to face if orient mode is 'face direction'
OrientMode.optional3: [face position] (vector) The point to face if orient mode is 'face point'

OverrideLightingOrigin.summary: Uses the entity origin as the lighting origin instead of the center of the entity's bounding area

OverrideSpawnflags.summary: Sets the spawnflags on this entity.  Used for patching.

PackRgba.summary: Packs rgba into a single int.

PathDistance.summary: Returns the approximate/semi-accurate path distance between two points or undefined if no path can be found.
PathDistance.optional1: <generatePathForAccurateDist> If set to true, a real path will be generated and the total length of the path segments will be returned.
PathDistance.optional2: <pathEnt> Only used when generatePathForAccurateDist is set. pathEnt will be used for generating a path, so it will respect the movement type. Only supported for actors.
PathDistance.optional3: <pathDistanceType> The method of pathdistance calculation. Two options are PATHDIST_APPROXIMATE(using getApproximateFuturePositions), PATHDIST_CORNERPREDICTED (using getCornerPredictor).
PathDistance.optional4: <maxCornerPredictions> Maximum number of cornerPrediction steps when PATHDIST_CORNERPREDICTED is used. Handles the edge case when cornerPrediction may not ever finish stepping the path all the way.

PathFixedOffset.summary: Sets a fixed offset for this vehicle to travel on a vehicle path.
PathFixedOffset.mandatory1: <offset> The offset to apply. A vector in the form (forward, right, up)

PathMode.summary: Set the path mode of this actor.
PathMode.mandatory1: <path mode> (const string) PathMode must be 'dont move', 'move allowed', or 'move delayed'
PathMode.optional2: [path delay] (float) Amount of time (seconds) to wait if path mode is 'move delayed'

PathMove.summary: Moves the entire path for this vehicle to the specific origin and angles.
PathMove.mandatory1: <node index> Which node to move to the new location (it moves the whole path not just this node).

PathMoveClear.summary: Clears the moved path for this vehicle.  If not cleared then all paths taken will be moved.

PathVariableOffset.summary: Sets a variable offset for this vehicle to travel on a vehicle path.
PathVariableOffset.mandatory1: <offset> The offset to apply. A vector in the form (forward, right, up)
PathVariableOffset.mandatory2: <time>, The interval at which to choose new offsets

PhysicsExplosionCylinder.mandatory2: <outer radius> The outer radius of the cylinder
PhysicsExplosionCylinder.mandatory3: <inner radius> The inner radius of the cylinder

PhysicsExplosionSphere.example: PhysicsExplosionSphere( origin, radius, radius, 5, max_damage, min_damage )
PhysicsExplosionSphere.mandatory2: <outer radius> The outer radius of the sphere
PhysicsExplosionSphere.mandatory3: <inner radius> The inner radius of the sphere

PhysicsJetThrust.mandatory2: <weapon facing dir> The weapon facing direction
PhysicsJetThrust.mandatory5: <angle limit> The angle limit for the thrust

PhysicsJolt.mandatory2: <outer radius> The outer radius of the jolt
PhysicsJolt.mandatory3: <inner radius> The inner radius of the jolt
PhysicsJolt.mandatory4: <impulse> The impulse created by the explosion

PhysicsLaunch.summary: Permanently turn this entity into a physics object with an intial force vector at the specified point.
PhysicsLaunch.optional1: [contact_point] The point to apply the initial force

PhysicsTrace.summary: Does a physics trace from start to end. Returns hit position
PhysicsTrace.example: trace = PhysicsTrace( start, end, ( 0, 0, 0 ), ( 0, 0, 0 ), self, PHYSICS_TRACE_MASK_VEHICLE_CLIP )
PhysicsTrace.optional3: [ignore entity] the entity to not consider

PhysicsTraceEx.summary: Physics trace, ignoring characters. Returns the full trace structure.
PhysicsTraceEx.optional4: [player clip] collide with the player solid mask instead of ai solid mask

PickupAmmoEvent.summary: Lets the client know a weapon was picked up
PickupAmmoEvent.optional1: [weapon]: Weapon to use for the event

PickupWeaponEvent.summary: Lets the client know a weapon was picked up
PickupWeaponEvent.mandatory1: [weapon]: Weapon to use for the event

PingPlayer.summary: Flashes a player on their teammate's compasses

PlaceSpawnPoint.summary: Raises the spawn point up to make sure it's not in the ground, then drops it back down into the ground.

PlayBattleChatterToTeam.summary: Play the sound alias as if coming from the entity, so that only one team can hear it

PlayerLinkTo.fullAPI: PlayerLinkTo(<linkto entity>,[tag],[view fraction],[right arc],[left arc],[top arc],[bottom arc],[use tag angles],[auto recenter])
PlayerLinkTo.summary: Attaches the player to an entity. Any entity rotation is added to the player's view, allow the player to look around. Rotating the parent entity/tag will not move the player's eye position, but only the player's view angles. Thus, the player's eye position is locked in place, always directly above the parent tag.
PlayerLinkTo.mandatory1: <linkto entity> The entity to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkTo.optional1: [tag] The tag to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkTo.optional2: [view fraction] How much the change in the tag's rotation effects the players view. Defaults to 0.
PlayerLinkTo.optional3: [right arc] Angle to clamp view to the right. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkTo.optional4: [left arc] Angle to clamp view to the left. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkTo.optional5: [top arc] Angle to clamp view to the top. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkTo.optional6: [bottom arc] Angle to clamp view to the bottom. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkTo.optional7: [use tag angles] Determines how the player's view will be tilted. 'False' (default) means that the orientation of the tag when the player is linked will appear flat to the player. Any rotation from that orientation will tilt the player's view. 'True' means that only a tag angles of (0,0,0) will appear flat to the player. Any rotation from (0,0,0) will tilt the player's view.
PlayerLinkTo.optional8: [auto recenter] Players view will auto recenter to the tag he is linked to. use tag angles must be true.

PlayerLinkToAbsolute.summary: Attaches the player to an entity. No view movement is allowed.  The player's eye position will remain fixed relative to the parent entity/tag. Thus, pitching and rolling will cause the player's eye position to move. (But the player entity always remains vertical)
PlayerLinkToAbsolute.mandatory1: <lockto entity> The entity to attach the player to
PlayerLinkToAbsolute.optional1: [tag] The tag to attach the player to

PlayerLinkToBlend.fullAPI: PlayerLinkToBlend(<parent>,[tag],[pos time],[pos accel time],[pos decel time],<time>,[angle accel time],[angle decel time])
PlayerLinkToBlend.summary: Only works if the player is currently linked to another entity and angles aren't locked. Lerps the current view angle constraints to the provided ones over the specified time.
PlayerLinkToBlend.mandatory1: <parent> The entity to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkToBlend.optional1: [tag] The tag to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkToBlend.optional2: [pos time] Lerp duration in seconds. Default is 1.
PlayerLinkToBlend.optional3: [pos accel time] Acceleration time. Default is 0.
PlayerLinkToBlend.optional4: [pos decel time] Decelaration time. Default is 0.
PlayerLinkToBlend.optional5: [angle time] angle Lerp duration in seconds. Default is <time>.
PlayerLinkToBlend.optional6: [angle accel time] angle Acceleration time. Default is 0.
PlayerLinkToBlend.optional7: [angle decel time] angle Decelaration time. Default is 0.

PlayerLinkToDelta.fullAPI: PlayerLinkToDelta(<linkto entity>,[tag],[view fraction],[right arc],[left arc],[top arc],[bottom arc],[use tag angles],[auto recenter])
PlayerLinkToDelta.summary: Attaches the player to an entity. Any entity rotation is added to the player's view, allow the player to look around. The player's eye position will remain fixed relative to the parent entity/tag. Thus, pitching and rolling will cause the player's eye position to move. (But the player entity always remains vertical)
PlayerLinkToDelta.mandatory1: <linkto entity> The entity to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional1: [tag] The tag to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional2: [view fraction] How much the change in the tag's rotation effects the players view. Defaults to 0.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional3: [right arc] Angle to clamp view to the right. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional4: [left arc] Angle to clamp view to the left. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional5: [top arc] Angle to clamp view to the top. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional6: [bottom arc] Angle to clamp view to the bottom. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional7: [use tag angles] Determines how the player's view will be tilted. 'False' (default) means that the orientation of the tag when the player is linked will appear flat to the player. Any rotation from that orientation will tilt the player's view. 'True' means that only a tag angles of (0,0,0) will appear flat to the player. Any rotation from (0,0,0) will tilt the player's view.
PlayerLinkToDelta.optional8: [auto recenter] Players view will auto recenter to the tag he is linked to. use tag angles must be true.

PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.fullAPI: PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta(<linkto entity>,[tag],[view fraction],[right arc],[left arc],[top arc],[bottom arc],[use tag angles])
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.summary: Similar to PlayerLinkToDelta(), but the player's body stays behind. The player's view attaches to the tag, and any weapon fire will also come from there.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.mandatory1: <linkto entity> The entity to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.optional1: [tag] The tag to attach the player to.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.optional2: [view fraction] How much the change in the tag's rotation effects the players view. Defaults to 0.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.optional3: [right arc] Angle to clamp view to the right. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.optional4: [left arc] Angle to clamp view to the left. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.optional5: [top arc] Angle to clamp view to the top. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.optional6: [bottom arc] Angle to clamp view to the bottom. Defaults to 180.
PlayerLinkWeaponViewToDelta.optional7: [use tag angles] Determines how the player's view will be tilted. 'False' (default) means that the orientation of the tag when the player is linked will appear flat to the player. Any rotation from that orientation will tilt the player's view. 'True' means that only a tag angles of (0,0,0) will appear flat to the player. Any rotation from (0,0,0) will tilt the player's view.

PlayerLinkedOffsetDisable.summary: If disabled, the linked player's orientation will not use their link offset.

PlayerLinkedSetUseBaseAngleForViewClamp.mandatory1: <enable> whether or not to enable using the base entity for the view angle clamp base.

After that, any rotation that the reference entity undergoes will affect the player.  Call this command again with undefined to turn it off.

PopulateScriptDebugMenu.mandatory2: <array> Array of menu item names for the debug menu.

PositionQuery_Filter_Directness.summary: Directness filter compute the direction information for each of the positions, relative to <origin> and <target>.
PositionQuery_Filter_Directness.mandatory1: <queryStruct> result from PositionQuery_Source function. each point contained here will have direction info computed.
PositionQuery_Filter_Directness.mandatory2: <origin> usually the position of AI before move.
PositionQuery_Filter_Directness.mandatory3: <target> the position AI is trying to approach.
PositionQuery_Filter_Directness.optional1: [resultVarName] by default the result directness value will be stored in pointStruct.directness, but can be override by this string.

PositionQuery_Filter_DistanceToGoal.summary: Calculate the distance to <entity>.goalpos. If the point is in <entity>.goalradius, the distance will be 0. the result is in pointStruct.distToGoal
PositionQuery_Filter_DistanceToGoal.mandatory2: <entity> the entity used to check goal."

PositionQuery_Filter_InClaimedLocation.summary: Check if the locations are inside other sentients claimed locations <entity>.inClaimedLocation.
PositionQuery_Filter_InClaimedLocation.mandatory2: <entity> the entity used to check goal."

PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.summary: Sight filter do sight check on each of the positions to a target position, and store the result in pointStruct.visibility (boolean).
PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.example: PositionQuery_Filter_Sight( queryResult, self.enemy.origin, self GetEye() - self.origin, self, 30, self.enemy );
PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.mandatory1: <queryStruct> result from PositionQuery_Source function. each point contained here will be used to check sight against <targetPosition>. "
PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.optional1: [offset] offset to add on each point before sight checking. this is usually something like: offset = ai GetEye() - ai.origin. default to (0,0,0). "
PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.optional2: [visibleBy_Ent] if defined, the corresponding sight check function on Actor or Vehicle will be called. Otherwise the checks just do simple bullet tracing."
PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.optional3: [numOfPassedToEarlyOut] if defined and bigger than 0, the filter will early out after found this many passed sight checks. this is useful if the points are pre sorted by score and we don't care about lower scored ones."
PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.optional4: [ignoreEnt] ignore entity during trace. if [visibleBy_Ent] is defined, it is already ignored internally. "
PositionQuery_Filter_Sight.optional5: [resultVarName] by default the results will be stored in pointStruct.visibility, but can be override by this string. "

PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.summary: Generate points around <origin>, between <minSearchRadius> and <maxSearchRadius> and within <halfHeight> height difference. For ground AI, the points will be on Nav Mesh; for flying AI, the points will be in Nav Volume. Only the points in pathable area will be kept. If the <origin> itself is in pathable area, all the points are guaranteed to be reachable from <origin>. If the <origin> is outside pathable area, all the points will still be in pathable areas surrounding <origin>, but reach-abilities cannot be guaranteed.
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.example: queryResult = PositionQuery_Source_Navigation( goalpos, self.radius, self.radius * 8, heightRadius, AI_GOAL_POINT_STEP, self, AI_GOAL_POINT_STEP * 0.2 )if ( queryResult.centerOnNav ){foreach( pointStruct in queryResult.data ){position = pointStruct.origindist = pointStruct.distToOrigin2D...}}
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.mandatory1: <origin> Position to start the search around.
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.mandatory2: <minSearchRadius> the minimum radius away from origin.
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.mandatory3: <maxSearchRadius> the maximum radius away from origin. can equal to minSearchRadius.
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.mandatory4: <halfHeight> the max height difference (both positive and negative) away from origin.
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.optional1: [reachableBy_Ent] if defined, it will be used to make sure all the points are reachable by this entity from <origin> (not the entity's current position). Can pass in a float number radius to get points on NavMesh. Can also pass in string 'navvolume_small' or 'navvolume_big' to get points on navvolume. Note passing in entity radius only gives rough estimate, not correct result.
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.optional2: [outerSpacing] if defined, the distance between points will be Lerped from <innerSpacing> to [outerSpacing], to create a gradual distribution.
PositionQuery_Source_Navigation.optional3: [distributionBias] (vec2) if defined, the density of the points will be changed gradually along the direction of this vector. the magnitude will be use as scalar, with 1 being the norm scale.

ScreenShake.mandatory2: <scalepitch> The scale of the shake in player's pitch. Max is 100; Accurate to 2 decimal places.
ScreenShake.mandatory3: <scaleyaw> The scale of the shake in player's yaw. Max is 100; Accurate to 2 decimal places.
ScreenShake.mandatory4: <scaleroll> The scale of the shake in player's roll. Max is 100; Accurate to 2 decimal places.
ScreenShake.optional1: <durationfadeup> Duration in seconds, included in base duration. Default is 0. Value of -1 means duration - durationfadeup.
ScreenShake.optional2: <durationfadedown> Duration in seconds, included in base duration. Default is -1. Value of -1 means duration - durationfadedown.
ScreenShake.optional3: <radius> The radius of shake, dampens as you move away from entity. Default is 0 = shake is global
ScreenShake.optional4: <frequencypitch> The scaled speed of the shake's pitch. Default is 1. Max is 100; Accurate to 2 decimal places.
ScreenShake.optional5: <frequencyyaw> The scaled speed of the shake's yaw. Default is 1. Max is 100; Accurate to 2 decimal places.
ScreenShake.optional6: <frequencyroll> The scaled speed of the shake's roll. Default is 1. Max is 100; Accurate to 2 decimal places.
ScreenShake.optional7: <exponent> The scale is multiplied by itself 'exponent' times to determine how the shake falls off. Default is 1 which is a linear falloff. Max is 100; Accurate to 2 decimal places
